inline_comments.message:
if a user runs the script without any params, he gets usage info and exit 1,
but if he runs the script as follows (without specifying port number):
docker-images-collector.sh -p
the script runs with the default port 8879. Maybe it would make sense to either return an info/error message to the user in such case or run with the default port if no parameters are specified? To make it consistent across different invocations.
Or implement a validation of the second parameter if -p is supplied.

As for the first sentence it has been also the case so far so my change didn't change anything in this scope.

As for the second it can be improved in some follow up change in future of course.
